topic: Is mathematics an invention of the human brain, or a fundamental discovery of the universe?
Will we ever be able to upload human consciousness to a computer?
Should artificial intelligence systems be granted legal personhood if they can demonstrate self-awareness?
What happens to human purpose when AI can perform all economically valuable work better than we can?
Can a truly private digital currency coexist with a functioning, taxable society?
Is zero-knowledge proof technology the ultimate solution to online privacy?
What is the ethical boundary of a 'hack back' policy for private corporations defending against cyber attacks?
AI will inevitably destroy humanity within our lifetime.
Cryptocurrency is nothing but a planetary-scale Ponzi scheme destroying the environment.
AI art isn't real art, and people who use it are just talentless hacks stealing from real artists.
Open-sourcing powerful AI models is equivalent to handing out nuclear launch codes to toddlers.
The AI 'safety' movement is just a disguised attempt by tech monopolies to regulate away all their competition.
99% of 'quantum computing startups' are complete snake oil selling impossible promises to gullible venture capitalists.
People who use 'quantum' to explain their spirituality are insulting actual physics.
Decentralization is a myth; the entire crypto ecosystem is controlled by a few centralized exchanges and whales.
NFTs were the greatest intellectual embarrassment of the 21st century.
People who get phished shouldn't get their money back; absolute stupidity shouldn't be subsidized.
Antivirus software is basically just a state-sanctioned malware racket that slows down your computer.
'White hat' bug bounty hackers are literally just extortionists with better PR.
Does the anthropic principle actually explain the fine-tuning of the universe, or is it just a philosophical cop-out?
If we manage to stop cellular aging, what are the ethical implications of a functionally immortal human race?
Is the simulation hypothesis a modern scientific theory, or just a digital recreation of religion?
Can entropy be reversed on a cosmic scale, or is the heat death of the universe an absolute certainty?
We should absolutely bring back extinct species like the woolly mammoth; ignoring the technology is cowardly.
The simulation hypothesis is just creationism for tech bros who took too many psychedelics.
Could extraterrestrial intelligence be so vastly different from us that we are currently looking at their technology and mistaking it for natural phenomena?

Laplace's Demon states that if we knew the precise location and momentum of every particle, we could calculate the entire future. Does quantum uncertainty destroy this idea, or is there a hidden deterministic layer?
People who cling to the idea of 'free will' just don't want to admit that they are essentially highly advanced biological algorithms.
The 'Prisoner's Dilemma' suggests that rational individuals might not cooperate even if it's in their best interest. Is this game theory the ultimate reason international treaties fail?

According to 'Godel's Incompleteness Theorems', any complex mathematical system contains true statements that cannot be proven. Does this guarantee the universe holds fundamental truths we can never verify?
The 'Dead Internet Theory' is already our reality; you are currently spending 80% of your time online arguing with bots and algorithmic engagement bait.
How might the James Webb Space Telescope change our understanding of the early universe?
What would happen to human society if we discovered a signal from an alien civilization?
How close are we to curing cancer, and what are the most promising current approaches?
What are the most promising approaches to slowing or reversing human aging?
What is consciousness, and can neuroscience ever fully explain it?
Could an artificial intelligence ever truly understand or experience emotions?
How might quantum computing change the fields of drug discovery, cryptography, and climate modeling?
What are the scientific limits of machine learning, and what problems will it never solve?
How should we define and test for artificial general intelligence (AGI)?
What role will brain-computer interfaces play in medicine and human enhancement?
How might advanced robotics change surgery, elder care, and disaster response?
What are the unintended consequences of algorithmic decision-making in science?
Could nanotechnology revolutionize medicine through targeted drug delivery?
What scientific fields will be most transformed by AI in the next decade?
How do we ensure AI systems used in scientific research produce reliable and reproducible results?
A self-driving car must choose between hitting an elderly pedestrian or swerving to endanger its young passenger. How should it be programmed, and who decides?
An AI can predict with 95% accuracy which employees will quit within six months. Should employers use this data, and should employees be told they are being analyzed?
A social media algorithm discovers it can reduce teen depression by 30% but only by heavily censoring content. Should the platform implement it without telling users?
You discover your company's AI hiring tool consistently rates candidates from certain zip codes lower. Your boss says it is just reflecting real performance data. What do you do?
Your company develops an AI that could replace 10,000 jobs but save the company from bankruptcy. Do you deploy it?
A brain-computer interface could cure your child's severe disability but requires collecting and sharing their neural data with a tech company. Do you consent?
A self-driving car must choose between hitting an elderly pedestrian or swerving into a wall, likely killing the passenger. How should it be programmed, and who is responsible for the outcome?
Advanced AI development should be paused until safety standards are agreed
AI systems should be legally required to disclose when they are interacting with a person
Autonomous weapons that select targets without human approval should be banned
AI-generated scientific research should be eligible for publication and patents
Facial recognition technology should be banned for government use
AI should be permitted to make medical diagnoses without physician sign-off
Training AI on copyrighted data without permission should be illegal
A sufficiently advanced AI should be granted legal personhood
Predictive policing algorithms should be prohibited
Brain-computer interfaces should be regulated as strictly as prescription drugs
